body: I think the root cause is the eager rebalance policy. We could try setting `session.timeout.ms` higher or switching to cooperative sticky rebalance.
Good call. I'll test cooperative sticky in the staging cluster today. If that reduces p99 by 50%+ we ship it.
Looks good. One nit: the metric name should match the existing convention `ridgeline.queue.*` not `rcl.queue.*`.
Fixed in latest commit. Updated all metric names to `ridgeline.queue.*` convention.
Draft PR up. Cap is set to 1000 in-flight jobs. Backpressure signal fires when queue depth hits 800.
Thanks Tom. Let me know when you're ready for review. I want to add a load test in staging before we merge — can you add a test job to the PR?
Staging upgrade is done and looking healthy. Will kick off the production node group upgrade on Monday morning during low-traffic window.
+1. Make sure the PDB is in place before starting the rollout. We got caught without one last quarter.
Started on this. Kings Peak and Uintas High Route waypoints are in. Will add Mirror Lake corridor this weekend.
PR merged. Ran the SLO burn rate report for the past 7 days and the 2% miss is gone — down to 0.1%.
I have spans working for `/compute.JobService/SubmitJob` and `/queue.ProducerService/Publish`. The remaining handlers are in progress.
Looking good. Make sure to tag the span with `rpc.service` and `rpc.method` per the OpenTelemetry semantic conventions.
Proposed fix: increase `REFRESH_TOKEN_GRACE_PERIOD_SECS` env var default from 2 to 30. Added a feature flag so we can tune per-environment.
30 seconds sounds right. Make sure the grace period is bounded and we still reject tokens used after revocation.
PR is ready for review. Uses Server-Sent Events under the hood to stream log lines. Works in both `--follow` and one-shot modes.
Nice implementation. One request: add a `--since` flag so users can replay the last N lines before following live, like `docker logs --tail N --follow`.
I'll implement a polling loop on the workspace status endpoint. Will use exponential backoff with a max wait of 10 minutes before failing.
For compacted topics we need to decide: do we implement at the client layer by key-deduplicating on publish, or do we need Redpanda-native log compaction? I lean native.
Agreed, native compaction is the right call. The client-layer approach would break ordering guarantees for changelog consumers.
We should add an allowlist for trusted proxy CIDRs in the config rather than trusting all X-Forwarded-For headers — otherwise this is trivially spoofable.
